item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just say that the Foundation has never observed an instance appearing. You don't need to explain why. It's unnecessary detail and bloats the description. It actually makes more sense to not explain it, since there's no reason the Foundation would know how they appear - they've never seen it happen!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That only applies to contests, and depends on the specific rules. This isn't a contest so you can edit whenever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, I get your criticism. For the first, I had an idea of a lightspeed camera that was off, then turned on, and didn't go fast enough to catch the appearance. I knew someone would ask

For the second, yeah trial and error makes sense, Im just stating that IS what happens, not if you know what to do or not

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the idea and its implications, but here's some things that got me kinda confused:

+ show questions
- hide questions

Instances anomalously materialize almost instantly within a standard mailing depository corresponding to a household at predicted faster-than-light speeds.

The delivery process is always unnoticed, and will not manifest if under surveillance of any known type or under direct eye contact of other sapient beings.

If you can never watch the delivery process, how can you predict that it can materialize itself at faster-than-light speeds?

subjects are able to change the physical construct of their bodies by applying slight pressure to an area of the body.

Do the subjects discover that functionality through trial and error? Because it doesn't seems a lot intuitive for me. It might be just me, but if I used this DVD, I would probably take it out of whatever reading device I used it, before I could figure out how it worked by myself.

Anyway, I'm just a newbie, and I'm not necessarily criticizing you, but rather just have some questions that maybe already have a reasonable explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, but i've always been confused, are you allowed to make major edits at any time, or is posting the final straw?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feedback!

+ show block
– hide block

The concept has real merit, but parts of this felt kind of awkward to me.

For one, this is a very long article that basically boils down to "a disc that, when viewed, lets a person change their body shape". You've got a lot of extraneous details that don't feel like they add much.

As well, I wasn't really sold on the whole "they magically appear" aspect of it; it didn't really fit with the rest of the article (for me at least).

Finally, there's awkward wording in a lot of places. For example:

Field agents must search residencies actively at the hours of 2 and 8 AM on a daily basis for SCP-3350 instances to prevent discovery by any nearby residents.

In addition to slightly clunky language, you never mention which residencies are being actively searched. I assume, from later in the article, that it's specifically this small town in Canada, but that should be made explicit in some way (even just by adding the phrase "in the affected area" or something after "residencies").

I want to like this one, but it needs cleaning up.

Best of luck!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nifty. I like that this didn't go where I expected. You have a stray SCP-xxxx in the description as well.

item.title: CREDITS FOR PHOTO
item.content:encoded:
